records.ety: [OE. dauben to smear, OF. dauber to plaster, fr. L. dealbare to whitewash, plaster; de- + albare to whiten, fr. albus white, perh. also confused with W. dwb plaster, dwbio to plaster, Ir. & OGael. dob plaster. See Alb, and cf. Dealbate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Daubed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Daubing.]

defs: To smear with soft, adhesive matter, as pitch, slime, mud, etc.; to plaster; to bedaub; to besmear.
quotes: She took for him an ark of bulrushes, and daubed it with slime and with pitch.

defs: To paint in a coarse or unskillful manner.
quotes: If a picture is daubed with many bright and glaring colors, the vulgar admire it is an excellent piece.
A lame, imperfect piece, rudely daubed over.

defs: To cover with a specious or deceitful exterior; to disguise; to conceal.
quotes: So smooth he daubed his vice with show of virtue.
I can safely say, however, that, without any daubing at all, I am very sincerely your very affectionate, humble servant.

defs: To put on without taste; to deck gaudily.
quotes: Let him be daubed with lace.

defs: To smear; to play the flatterer.
quotes: His conscience . . . will not daub nor flatter.

defs: A viscous, sticky application; a spot smeared or daubed; a smear.

quotes: Did you . . . take a look at the grand picture? . . . 'T is a melancholy daub, my lord.
